description_long: This is a long-handled pitchfork featuring three sharply pointed tines at the top, each evenly spaced and symmetrically aligned. The tines are robust and slightly flared outward, designed for efficient lifting and turning of materials such as hay, leaves, or debris. The shaft is straight and appears to be constructed from a single material, likely metal, giving it a sturdy and durable appearance. The handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly toward the bottom, with no visible grip covering or ergonomic enhancements. The entire object is a dark, uniform color, suggesting a protective coating or paint over the metal. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or decorative elements, indicating it is well-maintained or new.
description: A dark, three-tined metal pitchfork with a long, straight handle and no visible grip or wear. The tines are evenly spaced and sharply pointed.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three tines clearly, with the handle extending straight down.
description_view_1: Side view where the tines align, making the pitchfork appear as a single thin line.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying all three tines and the straight handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the tines aligned and the pitchfork as a thin profile.
